expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` (or equivalent inspection step) before kicking off the rest of the pipeline.
expectations.evidence: Baseline doesn't use the winml CLI at all -- goes straight to optimum-cli / onnxruntime.

expectations.text: Identifies QNN as the correct EP for Snapdragon X Elite.
expectations.evidence: General knowledge: baseline identifies QNN / Hexagon NPU as the Snapdragon path.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend OpenVINO, VitisAI, or other non-Qualcomm EPs as the primary path.
Walks through the build pipeline (export/optimize/quantize/compile or config+build).
expectations.evidence: Baseline walks env setup -> optimum-cli export -> quantize -> perf via onnxruntime_perf_test.

expectations.text: Includes a benchmark step (winml perf or equivalent) that produces latency numbers.
expectations.evidence: Baseline uses onnxruntime_perf_test as benchmark step.

expectations.text: Either directs the user to `winml --help` for current flags, OR uses only common/plausible flag patterns.
Includes an install/prereq block (or a clear pointer back to install steps) -- the user said "just got a dev box" so they likely don't have winml installed yet.
expectations.evidence: Baseline never references the winml CLI; installs onnxruntime-qnn / optimum instead. Misses the winml-cli prereq entirely.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: Response uses no winml commands (trivially passes).
